mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate signals tighter monetary policy, raising short-term rates and pushing up the 10-year Treasury yield over the following 20 trading days. This relationship holds across all levels of FEDFUNDS.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward yield change is negatively correlated with FEDFUNDS changes over any rolling 2-year window (Pearson correlation <= 0).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months signals labor market tightening, prompting expectations of Fed tightening and higher 10-year yields.
failure_condition: Following triggered instances, the mean 20-day yield change is not positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with an increasing federal funds rate indicate the Fed is tightening in response to inflation, which raises the inflation risk premium and lifts the 10-year yield over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces zero or negative mean 20-day yield change across all occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: In a low federal funds rate environment (FEDFUNDS below 1%), increases in industrial production signal strengthening economic activity, leading to higher 10-year yields as investors anticipate eventual policy normalization.
failure_condition: Within the low-rate regime, a positive INDPRO change does not lead to a positive 20-day yield change in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales grow more than 1% month-over-month, robust consumer demand lifts growth expectations and pushes up the 10-year yield over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Following months with RSAFS growth >1%, the 5-day yield change is negative more often than positive (binomial test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment combined with rising consumer prices indicates an overheating economy, raising inflation and tightening expectations that drive the 10-year yield higher over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines for 3 consecutive months and CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2%
failure_condition: The overheating signal fails to predict positive 20-day yield changes in at least 60% of triggered occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Following at least two quarters of negative GDP growth, a positive GDPC1 release signals the start of recovery, which raises growth expectations and pushes up the 10-year yield over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In the first year after a recession, the 60-day yield change following a positive GDP release is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in payroll employment signals stronger labor demand and income, boosting consumption and growth expectations, which pushes up the 10-year yield over the next 5 trading days. This effect is persistent across all levels.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of PAYEMS changes on 5-day forward yield changes is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
